response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack development skills and a diverse technical background, but his experience lacks depth in the core C++ and industrial automation domains required for this role. His expertise in secure development, event-driven architectures, and mentorship is valuable, but the role's emphasis on C++, multi-threading, and industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443) is not fully reflected in his resume.
strengths: Extensive experience with event-driven architectures, message brokers, and modular system design (e.g., GISCollective, Sociomantic).
Proven track record in secure development practices, mentorship, and technical documentation (e.g., workshops, Refactoring Dojos).
Strong problem-solving and cross-functional collaboration skills, with experience in agile methodologies and full-stack development.
concerns: Limited recent hands-on experience with C++ (last notable C++ role was at National Instruments in 2014–2015).
No explicit mention of industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443) or secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices.
Primary expertise lies in web/mobile technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than the role's core focus on C++ and industrial automation.
